topicDefinition.oneLiner: Compare features, pricing, and quality of the top AI video generators. Find the perfect tool for your needs.
boundaries: Must contrast multiple tier-1 models directly against each other.
Must evaluate models across different dimensions (speed, resolution, realism, price).
Excludes single-product reviews; focuses entirely on market comparisons.

howToChoose.title: Define Your Production Needs
howToChoose.desc: Are you creating fast social clips (prioritize Kling or Luma) or cinematic, story-driven B-roll (prioritize Sora or Runway)?

howToChoose.title: Check Native Resolutions
howToChoose.desc: Not all models generate in 4K. Kling 3.0 offers native 4K at 60fps, while Sora and Veo generally max out at 1080p for base generations.

howToChoose.title: Compare Multimodal Input Support
howToChoose.desc: If you need strict character consistency, look for models that allow multiple image references rather than just text prompts (like Seedance 2.0).

howToChoose.title: Evaluate API Costs
howToChoose.desc: If building an app, API costs vary wildly. Kling is currently the cheapest at roughly $0.029/sec, while Veo and Sora can be up to 5x more expensive.

sources.title: The Complete 2026 AI Video Generator Comparison
sources.excerpt: A highly technical specification comparison of the four leading AI video generation models of 2026.

sections.heading: Top Performers
sections.content: Kling AI 3.0 offers remarkable quality at competitive pricing, achieving native 4K resolution at 60fps. OpenAI Sora 2 delivers photorealistic results with physics-aware generation. Seedance 2.0 introduced unprecedented 12-file multimodal input.

toolMentions.bestFor: High-resolution volume and social media speed
proofSnippets: Kling 3.0 offers remarkable quality at competitive pricing, recently achieving native 4K resolution at 60fps—the first model to do so.

keyClaims: Delivers photorealistic results with physics-aware generation
pricingClaims: No free tier; requires ChatGPT Plus ($20/month)
proofSnippets: OpenAI Sora 2 delivers photorealistic results with physics-aware generation and the longest native clip duration (25 seconds).

toolMentions.bestFor: Native audio sync and enterprise API
keyClaims: Leads for developers with official API access
limitations: Limited preview access for UI users
proofSnippets: Veo 3.1 (Google) leads for developers with official API access and native audio generation capabilities.

toolMentions.bestFor: Maximum creative control via multimodal input
proofSnippets: Seedance 2.0 introduced unprecedented 12-file multimodal input for maximum creative control, supporting text, images, video, and audio combined in a single request.

notes: Incredibly dense comparison of the technical limitations of generation APIs.

sources.title: Best AI Video Generators 2026: Complete Comparison & Reviews
sources.excerpt: A consumer-friendly evaluation comparing UI ease of use and subscription values.

sections.heading: Top Performers
sections.content: Runway Gen-3 Alpha leads with exceptional motion quality and professional editing integration. Pika 2.0 provides an intuitive, fun interface ideal for social media creators.

toolMentions.bestFor: Broader creative suite and team workflows
keyClaims: Exceptional motion quality and consistent character coherence
proofSnippets: Runway Gen-3 Alpha leads with exceptional motion quality, consistent character coherence, and professional editing integration.

keyClaims: Intuitive, fun interface ideal for social media creators
proofSnippets: Pika 2.0 provides an intuitive, fun interface ideal for social media creators, with a free tier and Pro at $10/month.

notes: Adds consumer platform (Runway/Pika) comparison context to the technical model comparisons.
